pi_items.source: Web search: Wind turbine optimization research shows Cp of 0.35-0.40 is a realistic design goal for workable wind turbines
Web search: Small Wind Turbine Blade Design and Optimization research; 3-bladed turbines with TSR=7 achieve Cp 0.45-0.50. Value of 0.55 is realistic upper bound and below Betz limit (0.593)

Task description and web search: TSR=7 is the standard/optimal value for 3-bladed horizontal axis wind turbines (HAWT). Confirmed in Schmitz/Betz theory documentation and wind turbine design literature
Reasonableness verification: Tolerance of ±1.0 allows TSR range 6-8, which is the documented operating range for 3-bladed HAWTs
Unable to find typical QBlade v0.96.2 project file sizes in public documentation or research literature. Threshold is conservative (likely just prevents empty files)
Task description (task.json) specifies exact path. File extension .wpa confirmed as correct for QBlade v0.96 (verified via .wpa project import forum and environment documentation)
Task description (task.json) explicitly specifies 'Create a file /home/ga/Documents/projects/results.txt'

privileged_info_summary: The task is to design an aerodynamically optimized wind turbine rotor using QBlade v0.96.2 with a NACA 4415 airfoil. Target design parameters are verified: Tip Speed Ratio (TSR) of 7 is the standard value for 3-bladed turbines. Expected power coefficient (Cp) range of 0.35-0.55 is realistic—research confirms optimized 3-bladed turbines at TSR=7 achieve Cp 0.45-0.50, with 0.55 as a reasonable upper threshold below the Betz limit (0.593). NACA 4415 is a widely-used wind turbine airfoil (4% camber, 15% thickness). QBlade project file (.wpa) and text results file paths are as specified. Data is not synthetic; the task uses real software with real airfoil specifications to generate optimization results.
